name: Macallan 18 Sherry Oak
nose: Rich dried fruit, orange peel, cinnamon, dark chocolate and a whisper of aged oak. Extraordinary depth even before the first sip.
palate: Full-bodied and sumptuously smooth. Layers of Christmas cake, ginger, vanilla cream and espresso unfold slowly across the tongue.
finish: Exceptionally long. Warm sherry, toasted oak and a lingering sweetness that persists for several minutes.
verdict: One of the most complete sherry-cask expressions ever bottled. The Macallan 18 is the benchmark against which all others are measured.
author: James Whitfield, Head Curator

name: Laphroaig 18 Year
nose: Intense medicinal peat, iodine and seaweed, with a surprising sweetness of vanilla hiding underneath.
palate: Powerful, smoky and complex. Brine, tar and lemon zest, with a honeyed sweetness that tempers the smoke magnificently.
finish: Long, warming and exceptionally smoky. Seaweed and sweet smoke linger for an astonishing duration.
verdict: For lovers of Islay peat, the 18-year expression of Laphroaig reveals a dimension of sweetness and complexity that the younger editions only hint at.
author: Oliver Park, Senior Curator

name: Rémy Martin XO
nose: Jasmine, rose, and fresh fig intertwine with toasted almond and a distant note of Havana cigar. Impossibly elegant.
palate: Velvety and opulent. Candied orange, plum jam, warm oak and a touch of saffron. The mid-palate is extraordinary.
finish: Silken and enduring. Dried apricot and vanilla persist, fading into a memory of white flowers.
verdict: A benchmark XO that exemplifies everything the Grande Champagne terroir promises. Rémy's finest hour.
author: Claire Dubois, French Spirits Curator

name: Yamazaki 12 Year
nose: Delicate and precise. Tropical guava, peach, and subtle Mizunara oak spice, with a thread of vanilla and white flowers.
palate: Silky light-to-medium body. Yuzu, soft honey, a hint of clove from the Japanese oak. Immaculately balanced.
finish: Medium length, clean and refined. The oak spice lingers with dried citrus peel — elegant to the very last moment.
verdict: The gateway expression that introduced the world to Japanese whisky. Years later, it remains one of the most perfectly balanced single malts in any category.
author: Hana Mori, Asian Spirits Curator

name: Don Julio 1942
nose: Rich caramel, roasted agave and warm vanilla. Notes of dried mango and a subtle earthy mineral quality from the highland terroir.
palate: Full and smooth for an añejo. Butterscotch, baking spice, roasted agave and oak. The alcohol is remarkably well-integrated.
finish: Warm and medium-length. Sweet vanilla and toasted oak taper to a clean agave finish.
verdict: The bottle that elevated tequila's prestige globally. Don Julio 1942 remains the gold standard of premium añejo — luxurious, sippable, and deeply satisfying.
author: Lucia Reyes, Agave Spirits Curator

name: Diplomatico Reserva 12
nose: Toffee, dried apricot and vanilla dominate, with a background of leather and aged oak. Sweetness is luxurious but never cloying.
palate: Rich and dessert-like. Demerara sugar, chocolate truffle, orange peel and a whisper of tobacco. Pot-still character shines through.
finish: Long and warming with a clean sweetness. Vanilla and dark toffee fade slowly into a trace of dried fruit.
verdict: Venezuela's finest rum. Diplomatico Reserva Exclusiva demonstrates that aged rum can compete with the world's great spirits on every level.
author: James Whitfield, Head Curator
